root_text.an4.36:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā antarā ca ukkaṭṭhaṁ antarā ca setabyaṁ addhānamaggappaṭipanno hoti.
root_text.an4.36:1.2: Doṇopi sudaṁ brāhmaṇo antarā ca ukkaṭṭhaṁ antarā ca setabyaṁ addhānamaggappaṭipanno hoti.
root_text.an4.36:1.3: Addasā kho doṇo brāhmaṇo bhagavato pādesu cakkāni sahassārāni sanemikāni sanābhikāni sabbākāraparipūrāni;
root_text.an4.36:1.7: Atha kho bhagavā maggā okkamma aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle nisīdi pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.an4.36:1.8: Atha kho doṇo brāhmaṇo bhagavato padāni anugacchanto addasa bhagavantaṁ aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle nisinnaṁ pāsādikaṁ pasādanīyaṁ santindriyaṁ santamānasaṁ uttamadamathasamathamanuppattaṁ dantaṁ guttaṁ saṁyatindriyaṁ nāgaṁ.
root_text.an4.36:1.9: Disvāna yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.36:3.1: “‘Devo no bhavaṁ bhavissatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.36:3.2: ‘na kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, devo bhavissāmī’ti vadesi.
root_text.an4.36:3.3: ‘Gandhabbo no bhavaṁ bhavissatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.36:3.4: ‘na kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, gandhabbo bhavissāmī’ti vadesi.
root_text.an4.36:3.5: ‘Yakkho no bhavaṁ bhavissatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.36:3.6: ‘na kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yakkho bhavissāmī’ti vadesi.
root_text.an4.36:3.7: ‘Manusso no bhavaṁ bhavissatī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.36:3.8: ‘na kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, manusso bhavissāmī’ti vadesi.
root_text.an4.36:4.1: “Yesaṁ kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, āsavānaṁ appahīnattā devo bhaveyyaṁ, te me āsavā pahīnā ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.an4.36:4.2: Yesaṁ kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, āsavānaṁ appahīnattā gandhabbo bhaveyyaṁ … yakkho bhaveyyaṁ … manusso bhaveyyaṁ, te me āsavā pahīnā ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.an4.36:4.3: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, uppalaṁ vā padumaṁ vā puṇḍarīkaṁ vā udake jātaṁ udake saṁvaḍḍhaṁ udakā accuggamma tiṭṭhati anupalittaṁ udakena;
root_text.an4.36:4.4: evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, loke jāto loke saṁvaḍḍho lokaṁ abhibhuyya viharāmi anupalitto lokena.

translation_text.an4.36:1.1: At one time the Buddha was traveling along the road between Ukkaṭṭhā and Setavyā,
translation_text.an4.36:1.3: Doṇa saw that the Buddha’s footprints had thousand-spoked wheels, with rims and hubs, complete in every detail.
translation_text.an4.36:1.6: Surely these couldn’t be the footprints of a human being?”
translation_text.an4.36:1.7: The Buddha had left the road and sat at the root of a tree cross-legged, setting his body straight, and establishing mindfulness in his presence.
translation_text.an4.36:1.8: Then Doṇa, following the Buddha’s footprints, saw him sitting at the tree root—impressive and inspiring, with peaceful faculties and heart, attained to the highest self-control and serenity, like an elephant with tamed, guarded, and controlled faculties.
translation_text.an4.36:1.9: He went up to the Buddha and said to him:
translation_text.an4.36:2.2: “I will not be a god, brahmin.”
translation_text.an4.36:3.1: “When asked whether you might be a god, centaur, spirit, or human,
translation_text.an4.36:3.2: you answer that you will not be any of these.
translation_text.an4.36:3.9: So what then might you be?”
translation_text.an4.36:4.1: “Brahmin, if I had not given up defilements I might have become a god … a centaur … a spirit … or a human. But I have given up those defilements, cut them off at the root, made them like a palm stump, obliterated them so they are unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.an4.36:4.3: Suppose there was a blue water lily, or a pink or white lotus. Though it sprouted and grew in the water, it would rise up above the water and stand with no water clinging to it.
translation_text.an4.36:4.4: In the same way, though I was born and grew up in the world, I live having mastered the world, unsullied by the world.
translation_text.an4.36:4.5: Remember me, brahmin, as a Buddha.
translation_text.an4.36:5.1: I could have been reborn as a god,
translation_text.an4.36:5.2: or as a centaur flying through the sky.
translation_text.an4.36:5.3: I could have become a spirit,
translation_text.an4.36:5.6: they’re blown away and mown down.
translation_text.an4.36:6.2: to which water does not cling,
translation_text.an4.36:6.3: the world doesn’t cling to me,
translation_text.an4.36:6.4: and so, brahmin, I am a Buddha.”

variant_text.an4.36:6.2: Toyena nupalippati → toye na upalippati (pts1ed); toyena nupalimpati (mr)
